_comment: Income Protection (IP) Medical Underwriting Rules — IP-M-XXX prefixed rules for occupation-based disability, physical/cognitive demands, pre-existing conditions, mental health, and age-based risk assessment.

metadata.description: Medical underwriting rules for Income Protection insurance covering occupation-based disability criteria, physical and cognitive demands assessment, pre-existing condition impact, mental health considerations, and age-based IP risk factors.

rules.name: occupation_disability_threshold
rules.description: Occupation-based disability criteria for income replacement — different occupations have different disability thresholds based on the likelihood of being unable to perform their specific job duties.
rules.underwriting_notes: Professional and white-collar occupations receive own-occupation disability definition. Blue-collar and manual occupations use any-occupation definition with increasing load factors.

rules.name: physical_demands_assessment
rules.description: Physical and cognitive demands assessment — evaluates the physical requirements of the applicant's occupation to determine disability risk, including lifting, standing, walking, and fine motor skills.
rules.underwriting_notes: Higher physical demand occupations increase IP claim likelihood. Heavy and very heavy demands require detailed occupational description and may trigger medical examination.

rules.name: cognitive_demands_assessment
rules.description: Cognitive demands assessment — evaluates the mental and cognitive requirements of the applicant's occupation including decision-making complexity, concentration demands, and stress levels.

high.notes: Requires detailed review of stress and decision-making capacity

very_high.notes: Triggers mandatory medical exam and stress history review

rules.underwriting_notes: High cognitive demand occupations may have elevated risk for stress-related claims and mental health-related disability.

rules.name: pre_existing_condition_impact
rules.description: Pre-existing condition impact on ability to work — assesses how pre-existing medical conditions affect the applicant's capacity to perform their occupation and the likelihood of disability claims.

minor_conditions.notes: Minor pre-existing conditions with full recovery

major_conditions.notes: Major conditions with significant functional impact

terminal_conditions.notes: Terminal diagnoses are declined for IP cover

rules.underwriting_notes: Pre-existing conditions are assessed based on severity, recency, and treatment status. Conditions affecting ability to work are weighted more heavily.

rules.name: mental_health_considerations
rules.description: Mental health considerations for IP — evaluates the applicant's mental health history and its impact on ability to work, including depression, anxiety, stress-related conditions, and psychological trauma.

mild_episode.notes: Mild mental health episode, fully resolved

moderate_episode.notes: Moderate mental health episode with treatment

severe_episode.notes: Severe mental health episode requiring hospitalisation or intensive treatment

ongoing_treatment.notes: Ongoing mental health treatment — requires GP report

suicide_risk_history.notes: Suicide risk history — enhanced underwriting required

rules.underwriting_notes: Mental health conditions are a leading cause of IP claims. Enhanced underwriting including GP and psychiatrist reports may be required for significant history.

rules.name: age_based_ip_risk
rules.description: Age-based IP risk assessment — evaluates how the applicant's age affects their disability risk, recovery prospects, and the appropriate benefit period for their life stage.

18_25.notes: Younger applicants — lower disability risk but higher moral hazard

26_35.notes: Prime earning years — moderate disability risk

46_55.notes: Near retirement — elevated disability risk, longer benefit needed

56_65.notes: Close to retirement — high disability risk, short waiting period recommended

66_plus.notes: IP cover not available for applicants aged 66 and over

rules.underwriting_notes: Older applicants have higher disability risk and shorter remaining working life. Benefit period recommendations reflect appropriate coverage duration.

rules.name: disability_definition_classification
rules.description: Disability definition classification — determines whether the policy should use own-occupation, any-occupation, or groups-occupation disability definition based on occupation class and risk profile.

any_occupation.notes: Any-occupation definition for manual workers — lower premium but stricter claim criteria

rules.underwriting_notes: Disability definition significantly impacts claim likelihood. Own-occupation definitions pay even if the insured can work in a different occupation.

rules.name: residual_disability_assessment
rules.description: Residual disability assessment — evaluates eligibility for partial income replacement when the applicant can work but with reduced capacity due to injury or illness.

parameters.notes: Residual disability pays proportionally based on income reduction (20% minimum loss required)

rules.underwriting_notes: Residual disability benefit provides partial income replacement. Requires income verification and ongoing proof of income loss.

rules.name: medical_examination_trigger
rules.description: Medical examination trigger — determines when a paramedical examination, GP report, or specialist report is required based on cumulative medical risk factors.

no_exam_required.notes: No medical exam needed for low-risk profiles

paramedical_exam.notes: Paramedical examination including blood work and ECG

specialist_report.notes: Specialist report based on medical history — case-by-case

rules.underwriting_notes: Medical investigation requirements escalate with age, cover amount, and cumulative medical risk score.

rules.name: drug_alcohol_screening
rules.description: Drug and alcohol screening assessment — evaluates substance use history and its impact on IP disability risk, including dependency, misuse, and rehabilitation status.

past_dependency.notes: Past dependency — requires evidence of sustained recovery

current_dependency.notes: Current dependency — enhanced underwriting and possibly declined

illicit_drug_use.notes: Illicit drug use — elevated risk rating

rules.underwriting_notes: Substance use history significantly impacts IP risk. Current dependency may result in exclusion of substance-related claims or overall decline.
